title: abutilon claussenii
intro: Abutilon claussenii, also known as the yellow-flowering abutilon, is a species of flowering plant in the mallow family (Malvaceae). It is a shrub-like plant native to South Africa, and its medicinal properties are attributed to its roots, which are used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments. The plant is believed to have anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties, which can help to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and promote wound healing. Herbal preparations of Abutilon claussenii include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to alleviate symptoms of colds, flu, and other respiratory infections.
